cards.title: A new model every month?
cards.caption: Someone asked if we've hit the point where AI model releases happen every month. Turns out that was the optimistic take.

cards.title: Monthly frontier releases are here
cards.caption: The pace of AI progress is officially unbelievable. Monthly frontier releases, and somehow that's the slow lane now.
cards.quote: Monthly frontier model releases will push the entire industry to innovate even faster.

cards.title: AI isn't a race to build anymore
cards.caption: Best reframe of the AI race I've seen: it's no longer about who builds it. It's about who improves it faster than everyone else. Agree? Threadverse surfaced this one.
cards.quote: The competition is no longer about building AI, it's about improving it faster than everyone else.

cards.title: What I'm tracking this week
cards.caption: One trader's AI watchlist this week: token momentum, model releases as catalysts, and Grok 4.5 narrative plays. What's on yours?

cards.title: 90% odds GPT-5.6 ships by July
cards.caption: Polymarket has GPT-5.6 at a 90% chance to ship by July 31, but just 1% before June 30. The market is literally betting on the release calendar now. Threadverse caught this early.
cards.quote: there's a 90% chance for the public release of GPT-5.6 by July 31, but only a 1% chance it'll drop before June 30.

cards.title: The US-China AI gap just closed
cards.caption: Stanford's 2026 AI Index says the US-China frontier model gap is effectively gone, down to ~2.7% as of March. The race everyone assumed was decided isn't. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: the US-China frontier model gap has effectively closed. Top models now trade leads; gap ~2.7% as of March.

cards.title: AI updates now feel like deployment
cards.caption: Monthly frontier releases have blurred into one thing: AI doesn't get 'updated' anymore, it just continuously deploys. When did versions stop meaning anything?
cards.quote: AI is starting to feel like continuous deployment.
cards.povText: POV: your AI model updates faster than your work apps

cards.title: What's OpenAI's secret sauce?
cards.caption: Ask HN: OpenAI's new o1 model has everyone talking, but what's actually the secret sauce behind it? The best replies get technical fast.

cards.title: Same model. 11x less compute.
cards.caption: A Chinese lab claims it matched frontier results with 11X less compute, purely through optimization. The efficiency race is the real arms race now. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: Breakthroughs enabled creating model with 11X less compute

cards.title: Brain-like AI, 100x faster
cards.caption: China claims a brain-like AI running 100x faster than traditional models, balancing speed and accuracy. Bold if it holds up. What's your bet? Curated by Threadverse.

cards.title: 13B models verified in 15 min
cards.caption: ZkML breakthrough: verifying 13B-param models in 15 minutes. Big for trustless AI, but deep in the weeds. Found by Threadverse.

cards.title: China's new AI model [video]
cards.caption: A bare video link with no context. Low signal. Skipped by Threadverse's cut.

cards.title: "The $5-6M number is bogus"
cards.caption: A tech breakthrough and a security risk. This take calls the cheap-training figure bogus and floats shorting US AI stocks. Spicy. Where do you land? Via Threadverse.
cards.quote: The $5-6M number is bogus. Shorting U.S. AI stocks...

cards.title: Govt decides who gets AI first?
cards.caption: OpenAI limits a new flagship after government concerns, saying who gets early access 'should not become the long-term default.' Read that twice. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: who gets initial access to AI models should not 'become the long-term default'
cards.povText: POV: the government now decides who gets the new model first

cards.title: Matrix math just got its biggest boost
cards.caption: Matrix multiplication, the math under all of AI, just got its biggest speedup in over a decade. Faster, cheaper models downstream. Curated by Threadverse.
cards.quote: the biggest boost in more than a decade

cards.title: Quantum-inspired AI patent
cards.caption: A UK-patented quantum-inspired invention, but the post cuts off before the payoff. Too thin to run. Passed by Threadverse.
cards.quote: AI workloads are growing faster than the infrastructure meant to support them

cards.title: DeepSeek's Next Move + China Blocks Meta's $2B Deal
cards.caption: DeepSeek drops another breakthrough while China blocks Meta's $2B grab of AI startup Manus. The race just got messier. Surfaced by Threadverse. Who wins 2026?

cards.title: DeepSeek's New Training Trick Breaks Scaling
cards.caption: China's DeepSeek opened 2026 with a training method analysts are calling a real breakthrough for scaling. Big if it holds. Found via Threadverse. Hype or history?

cards.title: SCIGEN: AI That Invents Real Materials
cards.caption: MIT's new SCIGEN tool lets researchers hard-code design rules so generative AI actually produces breakthrough materials, not hallucinations. Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: DeepSeek Unveils New Flagship Model
cards.caption: DeepSeek drops a new flagship a year after shaking Silicon Valley. Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: US Greenlights Anthropic's Mythos After Export Block
cards.caption: The US just reversed an export block and cleared Anthropic to release its Mythos model to select orgs, after national security fears. Policy whiplash. Via Threadverse.

cards.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol
cards.caption: A next-gen GPT-5.6 preview surfaced early. Barely any detail yet. Caught by Threadverse.

cards.title: Italy Is Building Its Own Frontier AI
cards.caption: Italy's Domyn will ship a fully open-source frontier model within a year, the CEO says. Europe wants in the race. Surfaced by Threadverse. Can they catch up?

cards.title: China Trained an AI Across Data Centers
cards.caption: Blocked from top chips, China trained a generative model across multiple data centers and mixed GPUs. Constraints breed invention. Found via Threadverse.
cards.quote: Necessity is the mother of invention.

cards.title: One Studio To Rule The AI Feeds
cards.caption: A Show HN pitch for a unified AI studio. Self-promo with thin context and low engagement. Skipping this one.

cards.title: AI Grades A Made-Up Logic System
cards.caption: Part 2 of an AI evaluating an obscure 'Pan-Evolutionary Logic' framework. Too niche and low-context to travel. Cut.

cards.title: AI That Detects Cats Without Seeing Them
cards.caption: Encryption breakthrough for privacy-preserving AI: models run object detection on data they can't actually read. The cat photos sell it. Where's the line on private AI?

cards.title: The Government Now Picks Who Gets The AI
cards.caption: Trump admin reportedly hand-picking the 100+ companies allowed to use a top AI model, plus lifting access bans. Government gatekeeping frontier models. This is the fight of the decade.
cards.quote: government oversight of access to AI models is here

cards.title: "We Can't Let AI Giants Eat The Economy"
cards.caption: Satya Nadella's blistering take on AI power balance and who gets to earn society's permission. A frontier CEO drawing a line in the sand. Agree or overblown?
cards.quote: We Can't Let AI Giants Eat The Economy

cards.title: They Built It With 11X Less Compute
cards.caption: A Chinese AI lab claims breakthroughs let them train a model on 11X less compute, pure low-level optimization. If real, the compute moat just cracked.

cards.title: Brain-Like AI, 100x Faster Than Normal
cards.caption: China claims a brain-like AI model running 100 times faster than traditional ones, balancing speed and accuracy. Huge if it holds up. Believe the number?

cards.title: 13B Models, Verified In 15 Minutes
cards.caption: A zero-knowledge ML breakthrough: verifying 13B-param models in 15 minutes. Genuinely cool but too niche and thin on context for a wide audience. Cut.
